title: Install all dependencies
description: Install all dependencies from package.json. Modifies package-lock.json if needed. pnpm equivalent: pnpm install | yarn equivalent: yarn install

title: Install a package
description: Install and save a package to dependencies. --save is the default since npm 5.

title: Install dev dependency
description: Install a package as a devDependency. Use for build tools, linters, test frameworks — anything not needed in production.

title: Install package globally
description: Install a package globally. Available system-wide as a CLI command. EACCES error? Fix npm permissions instead of using sudo.

title: Uninstall a package
description: Remove a package and update package.json. Also removes from node_modules.

title: Clean install (CI/CD)
description: Deletes node_modules first, installs exactly from package-lock.json. Never modifies lock file. Always use this in CI/CD pipelines.

title: Update packages
description: Update all packages to the latest version within semver range defined in package.json. Won't break breaking changes.

title: List outdated packages
description: Shows which packages have newer versions available. Current, wanted (semver-safe), and latest columns.

title: Start dev server
description: Run the dev script from package.json. Typically starts Vite/webpack dev server with HMR. pnpm dev / yarn dev also work.

title: Build for production
description: Run the build script. Typically produces optimized output in dist/. Minified, tree-shaken, ready to deploy.

title: Preview production build
description: Serve the production build locally. Always test the actual build before deploying — dev and prod can behave differently.

title: Run tests
description: Run the test script defined in package.json. Usually maps to vitest, jest, or mocha.

title: Run linter
description: Run the lint script. Usually ESLint. Fix errors before committing — your CI will catch them anyway.

title: Create package.json (defaults)
description: Create a package.json with all defaults. -y skips the questionnaire. Good starting point for any Node.js project.

title: Create Vite + React TS project
description: Bootstrap a React + TypeScript project with Vite. The modern alternative to CRA. Fast HMR, ESBuild, zero config.

title: Create Next.js project
description: Bootstrap a Next.js app with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS. The go-to for full-stack React apps.

title: Run package without installing
description: Execute a package directly without global install. @latest ensures you always get the newest version.

title: Security audit
description: Scan dependencies for known vulnerabilities. Run this in your CI pipeline. Check the severity — not all issues need immediate action.

title: Auto-fix vulnerabilities
description: Automatically fix vulnerabilities within semver range. Safe — won't update packages past their allowed version range.

title: Force-fix vulnerabilities
description: Fix vulnerabilities even if it requires semver-major updates. May introduce breaking changes. Review the diff carefully.

title: Bump patch version
description: Increment patch version (1.0.0 → 1.0.1). Also creates a git tag. Use for bug fixes.

title: Bump minor version
description: Increment minor version (1.0.0 → 1.1.0). Also creates a git tag. Use for new backward-compatible features.

title: Bump major version
description: Increment major version (1.0.0 → 2.0.0). Breaking changes. Also creates a git tag.

title: Publish to npm registry
description: Publish your package to the npm registry. --access public is required for scoped packages (@org/pkg). Run dry-run first.

title: Simulate publish (dry run)
description: Preview what would be published without actually publishing. Shows file list and tarball size. Always run before the real publish.
